assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact-map.md explicitly flags 5 ambiguities from TC-9002: (1) 'Search should be faster' — no performance baseline or target defined, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' — no relevance criteria defined, (3) 'Add filters' — filter types and fields not specified, (4) 'Better UI' — out of scope (no frontend repo), (5) Non-functional requirements are vague ('fast enough', 'don't break existing functionality'). Each maps directly to vague language in the feature description. This exceeds the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: Impact-map.md Ambiguity 4 states: 'The "Better UI" requirement is marked as non-MVP and cannot be planned without design mockups or a frontend repository. The trustify-backend repository is a Rust backend service with no frontend code. This requirement is excluded from the current plan scope entirely. It should be revisited when a frontend repository is available and Figma mockups have been provided.' No task addresses UI changes.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks follow the template format with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify (and Files to Create where applicable), Implementation Notes, Reuse Candidates, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, and Verification Commands. Optional sections like API Changes and Documentation Updates are used only when applicable (task-3 has API Changes and Documentation Updates; task-4 has Dependencies). This matches the template's rules that optional sections should be omitted when not applicable.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All tasks with ambiguous requirements use the explicit label 'Assumption pending clarification:' - Task 1: 'Assumption pending clarification: We assume the goal is sub-500ms p95 response time for typical search queries.' Task 2: 'Assumption pending clarification: We assume relevance should be determined by PostgreSQL ts_rank or ts_rank_cd scoring...' Task 3: 'Assumption pending clarification: We assume filters should be added to the unified search endpoint (GET /api/v2/search) with the following filter parameters: entity_type...' The impact-map.md also labels each assumption as 'Assumption pending clarification' in all 5 ambiguity sections.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have a '## Repository' section with a single value 'trustify-backend': task-1-search-indexing.md line 1-2, task-2-search-relevance.md line 1-2, task-3-search-filters.md line 1-2, task-4-search-integration-tests.md line 1-2. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain: '## Target Branch' (set to 'main'), '## Description' (with detailed content), '## Acceptance Criteria' (with checkbox items), and '## Test Requirements' (with checkbox items). Verified in: task-1-search-indexing.md (lines 5,7,38,46), task-2-search-relevance.md (lines 4,7,39,47), task-3-search-filters.md (lines 4,7,48,57), task-4-search-integration-tests.md (lines 4,7,43,52).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All 'Files to Modify' paths exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs (line 17), common/src/db/query.rs (line 27), modules/search/src/service/mod.rs (line 91), modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (line 93), tests/api/search.rs (line 113). The 'Files to Create' path migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs follows the existing pattern from migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs (line 19-20) which is appropriate for a new file. No invented paths were found.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks' Implementation Notes reference specific paths and patterns: Task 1 references 'migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'migration/src/lib.rs' with specific patterns (GIN index, B-tree indexes, SeaORM migration). Task 2 references 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/error.rs' with specific patterns (ts_rank, PaginatedResults<T>, AppError). Task 3 references 'modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs', 'modules/search/src/service/mod.rs', 'entity/src/sbom.rs', 'entity/src/advisory.rs', 'entity/src/package.rs', 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', 'common/src/db/query.rs', 'common/src/model/paginated.rs', 'common/src/error.rs'. Task 4 references 'tests/api/search.rs', 'tests/api/sbom.rs', 'tests/api/advisory.rs' with specific patterns (assert_eq! pattern, PostgreSQL test database).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': task-1-search-indexing.md line 5, task-2-search-relevance.md line 4, task-3-search-filters.md line 4, task-4-search-integration-tests.md line 4.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 digest files exist and contain properly formatted digests: task-1-digest.txt: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:d54684190a8ba14d7b71c1373712f2832e319062292099ff3da49608916b9057' (64 hex chars verified), task-2-digest.txt: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:b199ddbc0cd4929ea996e358c2e0719b543f6f2bd78268f0d9b41d6a1d9d7b22' (64 hex chars verified), task-3-digest.txt: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:4e96b5293b53d83f401f857095ce182d9b15d3044fd142e6d1071af87565c1af' (64 hex chars verified), task-4-digest.txt: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:f10968c1a594385f20886f47abd0b9558a1c7ddfe72e0a5ed1541a8e3a212db0' (64 hex chars verified). All use the 'sha256-md:' prefix and contain exactly 64 lowercase hexadecimal characters.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task Implementation Notes contain the prescribed rationale format 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'. Grep for 'Applies:' across all task files returned zero matches. The tasks reference 'Per docs/constraints.md' sections (e.g., '§2 (Commit Rules)', '§3 (PR Rules)', '§5 (Code Change Rules)') but these are constraint references, not CONVENTIONS.md convention applicability rationales. There is no evidence of convention-applicability-rules.md being applied — no 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §...' lines with 'Applies:' rationale appear in any task. The convention-applicability-rules.md requires the format 'Applies: task modifies <matching file(s)> matching the convention's <scope signal>' which is entirely absent.
